# ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0005 - QUIC RFC 9001 Stateless Reset Disposition Matrix Architecture

## Purpose

Define the design boundary for the helper-owned stateless-reset formatting-failure branch after AEAD-limit terminal discard, keeping emission suppression exact when formatting inputs are malformed.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0007

## Design Summary

The runtime keeps stateless-reset formatting in a shared helper and treats invalid formatting inputs as a non-emitted branch. The endpoint helper remains the single place that formats a datagram, and the endpoint-host and listener-host paths continue to delegate through that helper rather than inventing their own formatting logic. This architecture keeps the retained-route floor from ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0004 intact while making the formatting-failure branch explicit and suppressive instead of success-biased.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicStatelessReset.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntimeEndpoint.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionEndpointHost.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicListenerHost.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0007.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicStatelessResetUnitTests.cs
- benchmarks/QuicStatelessResetBenchmarks.cs

## Data and State Considerations

The formatting branch uses only the helper inputs, not a broader route lookup. Token bytes, destination length, and retained version-profile snapshot are the relevant state inputs; if any of them are malformed, the helper returns a non-emitted result and the caller drops the response path. The endpoint and host shells should not cache a parallel formatting outcome.

## Edge Cases and Constraints

- Invalid token length must suppress emission.
- Undersized datagram length or destination space must suppress emission.
- An empty version-profile snapshot must suppress emission.
- Only a fully formatted datagram may be reported as emitted.
- The retained-route floor from ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0004 remains a subset of this matrix.

## Alternatives Considered

- Allow each host shell to format its own stateless reset datagram. Rejected because it would create divergent matrix behavior and duplicate validation.
- Fold malformed-format handling into the retained-route floor without a separate slice. Rejected because the failure branch is a distinct helper outcome and should remain traceable on its own.
- Treat any helper failure as an emitted reset. Rejected because it would fabricate responses from invalid inputs.

## Risks

- If helper-format failures are not kept separate from emitted outcomes, callers can overclaim response success.
- If host shells reconstruct their own formatting rules, the matrix can drift away from the shared helper.
- If the retained-route floor and the formatting-failure branch are merged, later trace slices can lose their boundary.

## Open Questions

- If a future ambiguity-specific route model appears, it should split into its own gap instead of broadening this helper failure slice.
